skill.name: frontend-design
skill.description: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
skill.summary: Guides AI assistants to design distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces.
skill.author: 92Bilal26

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported a hardcoded URL, weak cryptographic algorithm matches, and reconnaissance matches. Manual review found only Apache license text and frontend design guidance, with no executable code, network calls, cryptography, data access, or prompt injection attempts.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Static Alert Dismissed
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. LICENSE.txt line 4 contains the standard Apache License reference URL. It is plain license text, not code that performs a network request or sends data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears inside Apache License text and no executable network API is present. This is strong evidence that the network alert is documentation only.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Alerts Dismissed
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged LICENSE.txt and SKILL.md lines contain natural-language words from license terms and frontend design guidance. No hashing, encryption, weak algorithm choice, or cryptographic API usage appears in these files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed files are license prose and markdown instructions for visual design. There is no code path where a weak cryptographic primitive could be selected or executed.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Static Alerts Dismissed
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md lines 7, 30, and 34 discuss design quality, font choices, and visual details. They do not instruct the assistant to enumerate files, inspect the host system, collect environment data, or run reconnaissance commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious terms are used in frontend design context only. No commands, filesystem enumeration, or environment inspection instructions are present.

content.value_statement: Generic AI interfaces often look interchangeable and lack product context. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward polished frontend work with a clear visual direction.
actual_capabilities: Guides frontend implementation for components, pages, applications, and web interfaces.
Prompts the assistant to choose a clear aesthetic direction before coding.
Encourages production-grade HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, Vue, or similar frontend code.
Emphasizes typography, color, motion, spatial composition, and visual detail.
Discourages generic layouts, common font choices, and overused AI-style gradients.
Aligns implementation complexity with the selected visual concept.
limitations: It provides design guidance, not a standalone design system or component library.
It does not include executable scripts, templates, or reusable source files.
Generated output quality depends on the user's requirements and the host AI model.
It does not verify accessibility, browser compatibility, or performance automatically.

use_cases.title: Prototype a Product Screen
use_cases.description: Create a memorable dashboard, settings page, or workflow screen that avoids generic visual patterns.

use_cases.title: Build a Polished Web Component
use_cases.description: Turn a component request into working frontend code with typography, color, spacing, and motion decisions.

use_cases.title: Explore Visual Directions
use_cases.description: Generate contrasting interface concepts for the same brief before committing to an implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Single Component
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one polished UI element.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-design skill to create a distinctive pricing card for a developer tool. Include responsive HTML and CSS.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Product Page
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a complete page.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-design skill to build a landing page for an analytics product. Choose a bold visual direction and implement the first screen.

prompt_templates.title: Revise an Existing Interface
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request with existing code or screenshots.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-design skill to improve this settings page. Keep the structure, but make the typography, spacing, color, and interactions feel intentional.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Multi-State App View
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a production-like interface.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-design skill to design and implement a project management board with empty, loading, active, and error states. Make it visually distinctive and responsive.

output_examples.input: Build a dashboard for a climate data product.
output_examples.output: The assistant selects a scientific editorial direction, then describes and implements a dense dashboard with refined charts, restrained motion, and strong data hierarchy.

output_examples.input: Create a checkout flow for a boutique furniture store.
output_examples.output: The assistant designs a warm retail interface with careful typography, product-focused layout, responsive form states, and subtle interaction feedback.
The assistant replaces the default layout with a context-specific first viewport, stronger type choices, improved spacing, and a clear visual concept.

best_practices: Provide the product context, audience, target platform, and any framework constraints.
Ask for a specific aesthetic direction when brand requirements already exist.
Review generated interfaces for accessibility, responsiveness, and production constraints before release.
anti_patterns: Do not request a polished result without explaining the product, user, or page purpose.
Do not accept decorative effects that reduce readability or task completion.
Do not use the skill as a substitute for final design review or usability testing.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create distinctive frontend components, pages, and applications with stronger visual direction and implementation detail.

faq.question: Does it include ready-made code files?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a markdown instruction file that guides an AI assistant during frontend generation.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Manual review found no executable code, prompt injection, data access, or dangerous behavior.

faq.question: Can it enforce accessibility requirements?
faq.answer: No. It can guide accessible design choices, but users should still test accessibility separately.

faq.question: When should I use this skill?
faq.answer: Use it when you want frontend output that feels intentional, specific to the brief, and less generic.
